rxn_class_summary: Catalyst-free electrochemical bromochlorination of alkenes using bromide as Br source and hexachloroethane (C2Cl6) as Cl source via paired electrolysis
electrochemical_role: Anodic two-step oxidation of Br- to Br+ cation; simultaneous cathodic reduction of C2Cl6 to Cl- anion and C2Cl4

key_intermediates.name: chloride anion (from cathodic reduction of C2Cl6)
dibromide product 5 (from Br- attack on bromonium)

electron_flow: At the anode, Br- undergoes two-step oxidation to Br+, which adds to the alkene to form a bromonium ion M; chloride, generated at the cathode by reduction of C2Cl6 (yielding Cl- and C2Cl4), opens M to give the bromochloride product 2 (Path I). In a parallel Path II, Br- opens M to dibromide 5, which is then converted to 2 by Cl-.

probe_reagents.name: MeOH
probe_reagents.observation: Bromoalkoxylation product 6a formed in 21% yield, supporting bromonium intermediate

probe_reagents.name: tBuOH
probe_reagents.observation: Bulky alcohol gave only trace bromoalkoxylation (6b) and bromochloride 2a in 56%, consistent with bromonium intermediate

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Significant yield decrease of 2a, but no BHT adducts detected by HRMS

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Significant yield decrease of 2a, no TEMPO adducts observed

probe_reagents.name: (1-cyclopropylvinyl)benzene 1p
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 7p obtained in 50% yield, suggesting involvement of a Br radical or cation

probe_reagents.name: bromochloride 2a (as substrate)
probe_reagents.observation: Converts to dichloride 4a (up to 18% with nBu4NCl); 8% without electricity, confirming interconversion

probe_reagents.name: dibromide 5a (as substrate)
probe_reagents.observation: Converts to bromochloride 2a (up to 53% with nBu4NCl) and small amounts of 4a, confirming 5a is on-path intermediate

notes: CV showed Br- oxidation (E1/2 = 0.98 V and 1.4 V) easier than styrene (2.06 V), indicating Br- is oxidized first rather than direct alkene oxidation. CV also confirmed C2Cl6 reduction at -1.46 V as the Cl source. Kinetics show dibromide 5a forms first, then converts to bromochloride 2a as Cl- accumulates; dichloride 4a forms only later from over-chlorination. Radical trap experiments suppress reaction but give no adducts, while cyclopropyl clock opens — the authors favor a cationic bromonium pathway but acknowledge possible Br radical/cation character.
